THROAT. ► The neck becomes thick when speaking loud, [4] . [260.] ► The post-mortem showed a separable orange-yellow patch on the uvula and arch of the palate, consisting of a thin but firm membrane easily detached; this patch was confluent with one on the posterior wall of the pharynx, and extended increasing in thickness into the sophagus and trachea; the mucous membrane under this membrane was hyperæmic but not swollen; the epiglottis was covered with a thick false membrane, the mucous membrane eroded, dark-red, with many swollen pustules, the submucous membrane dematous but relaxed; the ventriculi Morgagnii closed by swelling and covered with a similar false membrane; the mucous membrane here was also intensely red and injected, a similar distinct membrane on the vocal cords, and a portion of the larynx beneath; the trachea free from the membrane; the whole extent of the bronchi, even to their finest divisions, inflamed and filled with tenacious mucus; the mucous membrane not diminished but greatly swollen, injected, and showing on every section numerous small points of (apparent) pus, which however might be considered masses of mucus; a high degree of acute emphysema between all the lobules of the lungs. The whole sophagus from the pharynx to the stomach was covered with a continuous false membrane, orange-colored, firm and detachable; the submucous connective tissue contained here and there phlegmonous purulent spots, [64] . ► Increased secretion of mucus in the throat, with rough voice, [2] . ► Subjective. ► Dryness of the throat, [45] . ► Dryness, burning, and constriction in the throat, [50] . ► Pain low down in the throat, [45] . ► Burning in the throat, [11] . ► Transient burning in the throat, [8] . ► Biting burning in the throat, extending into the air-passages, [10] . ► The sea-air seemed to burn his throat, [56] . ► Feeling of fulness in the throat (sixth day), [1] .
